name: APT29 (Cozy Bear)
description: Russian state-sponsored group targeting government and critical infrastructure. Known for SolarWinds supply chain attack.

name: APT28 (Fancy Bear)
description: GRU-attributed group known for targeting political organizations and elections worldwide.

name: Lazarus Group
description: DPRK-affiliated group responsible for WannaCry ransomware and financial institution heists.

name: FIN7
description: Financially motivated group targeting hospitality, restaurant, and retail sectors for payment card theft.

name: REvil (Sodinokibi)
description: RaaS group responsible for high-profile ransomware attacks including Kaseya VSA supply chain attack.

name: Cobalt Strike C2 Beacon
description: Commercial penetration testing tool widely abused by threat actors as C2 framework.

name: Mimikatz Credential Dumping
description: Open-source credential extraction tool used for dumping Windows credentials from LSASS.

name: Log4Shell Exploitation Pattern
description: JNDI injection pattern used to exploit Log4j vulnerability for remote code execution.

name: Emotet Malware Distribution
description: Modular banking trojan used as initial access broker, dropping additional payloads like Trickbot and Ryuk.

name: PrintNightmare Exploitation
description: Windows Print Spooler privilege escalation and RCE vulnerability exploitation patterns.

name: ProxyLogon Exchange Attack
description: Microsoft Exchange Server pre-authentication SSRF leading to RCE.

name: Golden Ticket Kerberos Attack
description: Forging Kerberos ticket-granting tickets using KRBTGT hash for persistent domain access.

name: DCSync Attack
description: Impersonating Domain Controller to request credential replication using DRSUAPI protocol.

name: BloodHound AD Enumeration
description: Active Directory attack path enumeration tool used to identify privilege escalation paths.

name: Ransomware Pre-deployment Indicators
description: Common behaviors observed in the 24-72 hours before ransomware deployment.

name: DNS Tunneling Data Exfiltration
description: Using DNS queries to exfiltrate data in encoded subdomains of attacker-controlled domains.
ioc_patterns: high volume DNS queries to single domain

name: Living Off The Land Binaries (LOLBins)
description: Using legitimate system binaries to execute malicious actions, evading detection.

name: NTLM Relay Attack
description: Capturing and relaying NTLM authentication to authenticate to other services.

name: Supply Chain Compromise Pattern
description: Indicators of software supply chain attacks, including build system compromise and trojanized updates.

name: Credential Stuffing Attack
description: Automated use of leaked credential pairs against authentication endpoints.

name: Web Shell Indicators
description: Common web shell detection patterns across PHP, ASPX, and JSP variants.

name: Cloud Resource Abuse
description: Cryptomining and resource abuse following cloud environment compromise.

name: Insider Threat — Data Staging
description: Behavioral indicators of insider threat data exfiltration activity.
ioc_patterns: cloud storage uploads from corp network

name: Pass-the-Hash Lateral Movement
description: Using captured NTLM hashes to authenticate without knowing plaintext passwords.

name: Spear Phishing with Weaponized Documents
description: Targeted phishing emails containing macro-enabled or exploit-laden Office documents.
ioc_patterns: Office documents with macros from email

name: Remote Desktop Protocol Brute Force
description: Automated attempts to brute force RDP credentials on internet-facing systems.
ioc_patterns: RDP login failures from external IPs

name: Exfiltration via HTTPS to Cloud Storage
description: Data exfiltration using HTTPS to legitimate cloud storage services to evade detection.
ioc_patterns: large PUT requests to cloud services

name: Kerberoasting
description: Requesting service tickets and cracking them offline to recover service account passwords.

name: Fileless Malware — PowerShell In-Memory
description: Execution of malicious code entirely in memory without writing to disk.

name: Azure AD / Entra ID Attack Patterns
description: Cloud identity attack patterns targeting Azure Active Directory.
